footer_legal_notice: PlayStation and PS Vita are trademarks of Sony Interactive Entertainment Inc. The emulator is not related to or endorsed by Sony, or derived from confidential materials belonging to Sony.
home_description: The world's first functional PlayStation Vita emulator.
home_vita3k_description: Vita3K is the world's first functional experimental open-source PlayStation Vita emulator for Windows, Linux, macOS and Android.
home_legal_notice: Please note that the purpose of the emulator is not to enable illegal activity.
home_blog_description: We have a blog where we irregularly publish detailed reports with the latest progress in our work. Come to read what's new!
home_the_emulator_can_run_some_commercia: The emulator can run some commercial games!
home_several_homebrew_games_are_also_sup: Several homebrew games are also supported. Check the homebrew compatibility list for further details.
home_ko_fi_page: If you would like to show your appreciation and even help fund development, the project has a Ko-fi Page.
quickstart_get_started: Get started with Vita3K and play your favorite PSVita games!
quickstart_hardware_requirements_desc: The emulator performance and accuracy varies depending on your hardware. We cannot guarantee it will perform well if your PC barely meets the minimum requirements. For the best experience make sure you're within the recommended requirements as most of the reported games are tested with such requirements.
quickstart_x86_64_cpu_with_avx: x86_64 CPU with the AVX instruction set
quickstart_microsoft_redistributable_des: If you're having trouble running Vita3K and it complains about VCRUNTME140_1.dll was not found, download and install the Visual C++ 2015-2022 Redistributable.
quickstart_operating_system_desc: You need to be running a 64-bit operating system in order for Vita3K to work.
quickstart_firmware_desc: Some games require the system modules be present for Vita3K to (low level) emulate them. This can be done by installing the PS Vita firmware through Vita3K.
quickstart_firmware_download: The firmware can be downloaded from the official PlayStation website.
quickstart_font_firmware_desc: There's also an additional firmware package that contains the system fonts that needs to be installed. The font firmware package can be downloaded straight from the PlayStation servers.
quickstart_could_not_get_url: But we couldn't get the URL for it, so you will have to find it yourself. Or in the discord server, using the -firmware command.
quickstart_install_both_firmware_package: Install both firmware packages using the File > Install Firmware menu option.
quickstart_managing_modules_desc: System modules can be managed in the Configuration > Settings > Core tab of the emulator, we recommend Modules Mode > Automatic. And if you have doubts some modules are causing crashes you can try to remove them.
compatibility_emulator_undergoing_change:
The emulator is currently undergoing great changes of its backend which are greatly improving compatibility with games.
This compatibility list doesn't reflect the state of the emulator anymore and everyone interested in the emulator is
encouraged to re-test their games with the latest emulator builds every few weeks to update the compatibility reports.
compatibility_nothing_desc: Games that don't boot at all, not displaying an initial black screen with a FPS counter.
compatibility_bootable_desc: Games that boot but display nothing more than a black screen with a FPS counter.
compatibility_intro_desc: Games that display any image on screen but doesn't reach to menus.
compatibility_menu_desc: Games that get into menu but can't enter Ingame.
compatibility_ingame_minus_desc: Games that go ingame but have major issues that prevents it from going further early on.
compatibility_ingame_plus_desc: Games that go far ingame but have glitches that render the game unplayable or have non-playable performance.
compatibility_playable_desc: Games that can be played from start to finish with playable performance and no game breaking glitches.
